messages.content: in my kotlin app i want to be able to display images in a recyclerview. so, i already have a prepared list of image locations - locations of the file in the internal storage. i want to get the image at the specified location, and insert it into my recyclerview. i want to do it without using any external libraries

To display images in a RecyclerView in Kotlin, you can use the following steps:

1. Create a layout for your RecyclerView item that includes an ImageView:

   ```xml
   <ImageView
       android:id="@+id/imageView"
       android:layout_width="match_parent"
       android:layout_height="wrap_content"
       android:scaleType="centerCrop"
       android:layout_margin="8dp"/>
   ```

2. In your RecyclerView adapter, create a ViewHolder that has a reference to this ImageView:

   ```kotlin
   class MyViewHolder(itemView: View) : RecyclerView.ViewHolder(itemView) {
       val imageView: ImageView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.imageView)
   }
   ```

3. In your adapter's onCreateViewHolder() method, inflate the item layout:

   ```kotlin
   override fun onCreateViewHolder(parent: ViewGroup, viewType: Int): MyViewHolder {
       val itemView = LayoutInflater.from(parent.context)
           .inflate(R.layout.recycler_view_item, parent, false)
       return MyViewHolder(itemView)
   }
   ```

4. In your adapter's onBindViewHolder() method, retrieve the image at the specified location and set it to the ImageView:

   ```kotlin
   override fun onBindViewHolder(holder: MyViewHolder, position: Int) {
       val imageFile = File(imageLocations[position])
       val bitmap = BitmapFactory.decodeFile(imageFile.path)
       holder.imageView.setImageBitmap(bitmap)
   }
   ```

   Note that `imageLocations` is the list of file locations for your images.

That's it! Now, your RecyclerView should display images for each item.
so, all of those should go into a class, which is the adapter?

`MyViewHolder` is a custom `ViewHolder` class that you define, which represents a single item view in your `RecyclerView`. It inherits from `RecyclerView.ViewHolder` and contains a reference to the `ImageView` in your layout, which is used for displaying the image.

Here's an example of how you can define your `MyViewHolder` class:

```kotlin
class MyViewHolder(itemView: View) : RecyclerView.ViewHolder(itemView) {
    val imageView: ImageView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.imageView)
}
```

The `MyViewHolder` class takes a single parameter, which is an instance of the `View` that represents the item view in your `RecyclerView`. In the constructor, you can retrieve a reference to the `ImageView` from the layout using `findViewById()`. This reference is stored in a member variable called `imageView`, which you can use later to set the image for the item view in the `onBindViewHolder()` method.

how can i get the image in my new activity?

To get the image in your new activity, you need to pass it from your `MyActivity` or `MyFragment`, which contains the `RecyclerView`, to your `FullScreenImageActivity`.

Here's how you can pass the image bitmap:

1. In your `MyActivity` or `MyFragment`, when the user clicks an image in your `RecyclerView`, create an intent to launch your `FullScreenImageActivity` and attach the image bitmap as an extra:

   ```kotlin
   val imageFile = File(imageLocations[position])
   val bitmap = BitmapFactory.decodeFile(imageFile.path)
   val intent = Intent(this@MyActivity, FullScreenImageActivity::class.java)
   intent.putExtra("image", bitmap)
   startActivity(intent)
   ```

2. In your `FullScreenImageActivity`, retrieve the bitmap from the intent using the extra key you used to attach it:

   ```kotlin
   val bitmap = intent.getParcelableExtra<Bitmap>("image")
   ```

3. Finally, display the bitmap in an `ImageView` in your `FullScreenImageActivity`.

   ```kotlin
   // Get a reference to the ImageView in your layout
   val imageView = findViewById<ImageView>(R.id.image_view)
   
   // Set the bitmap to the ImageView
   imageView.setImageBitmap(bitmap)
   ```

That's it! Now, when the user taps an image in your `RecyclerView`, `MyActivity` or `MyFragment` launches the `FullScreenImageActivity`, where the image is displayed in full-screen mode.
